_comment: Per-tool editorial input to scripts/compose-tool.py. This tool was authored (2026-07-14) before the compose pipeline was promoted (I4); this config onboards it to scripts/compose-tool.py so its audit and derived artifacts are reproducible. The script computes structural audit flags mechanically; this file supplies the topic-specific substantive caveats, the non-leaf evidence_quality judgements, leaf-quality overrides (with reason), and audit notes. Computed-flag wording is normalised to the canonical script.
advocacy_note: none used. The After Babel critiques of Ferguson are carried inside caveats as a documented dispute, not as findings.

n_trend_attrib.reason: Mechanically two-sided (S1/C2) but the single FOR cluster (twenge) carries both FOR findings, and scaling an individual-level association into the principal population driver requires unearned assumptions. This is the map's most conflation-prone claim; the honest state is thin, not well-evidenced.

n_assoc: mostly correlational — causal interpretation is not licensed
self-reported use dominates — device-log associations run weaker
n_assoc_magnitude: mostly correlational — causal interpretation is not licensed
self-reported use dominates — device-log associations run weaker
Evidence base is Global-North-heavy (>70% of studies; 0% Africa) and the pooled association is null in the Global South (Ghai et al. 2023). External validity to other settings is uncertain.
n_assoc_causal: strongest designs use 2000s college students and adult samples — transportability to today's adolescents not established
Causal designs use Western (US/UK) samples; no qualifying LMIC causal design located. Evidence base is high-income-heavy; external validity to other settings is uncertain.
n_assoc_hetero: mostly correlational — causal interpretation is not licensed
Subgroup evidence (girls, early adolescence) is Anglosphere/Nordic; no LMIC subgroup replication located. Evidence base is high-income-heavy; external validity to other settings is uncertain.
n_assoc_benefits: single-source FOR: NAS-2024 is the only qualifying FOR cluster
Benefit evidence centres on Western marginalized-youth samples; no LMIC benefit study located. Evidence base is high-income-heavy; external validity to other settings is uncertain.
n_trend: Anglosphere/Nordic-centric evidence — external validity not established
n_trend_real: no full AGAINST located — artifact hypothesis carried via COMPLICATES only
Anglosphere/Nordic-centric evidence — external validity not established

GBD 2021 (F-geo-3) shows the rise is high-income-concentrated (fastest in North America) with global rates broadly stable; the deterioration is not established as global. External validity to other settings is uncertain.
n_trend_attrib: correlational timing evidence — causal interpretation is not licensed

GBD 2021 (F-geo-2) shows social media diffused globally while the deterioration did not (global rates stable; several LMIC regions declining). Evidence base is high-income-heavy; external validity to other settings is uncertain.
n_mech: screens ≠ social media — much of this literature measures devices generally
n_mech_sleep: screens ≠ social media — both meta-analyses measure devices/screens generally
single cluster per direction — two strong meta-analyses, one each way
Sleep meta-analyses are high-income/general-screen; no LMIC social-media-specific sleep study located. Evidence base is high-income-heavy; external validity to other settings is uncertain.
n_policy: fast-moving policy literature — several key sources are 2024–2026 working papers
n_policy_school: working-paper-heavy FOR side — peer review pending
design mismatch: causal ban-timing studies vs cross-sectional policy comparison
Ban-outcome evidence is Nordic/UK/US; no LMIC school-ban evaluation located. Evidence base is high-income-heavy; transferability to other settings is uncertain.
n_policy_deact: adult samples throughout — adolescent transportability assumed, not shown
Deactivation RCTs are Western adult samples; no LMIC deactivation trial located. Evidence base is high-income-heavy; external validity to other settings is uncertain.
n_policy_ban: aus-ban-eval supplies both findings here (single source)
three-month early evidence during contested rollout — measures use, not mental health
The only ban evaluation is Australian; no LMIC age-ban evidence exists. Evidence base is high-income-heavy; external validity to other settings is uncertain.

audit_notes.n_assoc_magnitude: Geographic (F-geo-1): Ghai et al. 2023 pooled r=.14 overall but positive/significant only in the Global North and null in the Global South, with >70% of studies Global North (0% Africa). Author overlap: Ghai shares an author (Orben) with the orben-przybylski cluster also carried AGAINST here, so the two AGAINST clusters are NOT fully independent — flagged, not treated as clean corroboration.
audit_notes.n_assoc_hetero: Three independent clusters support concentration of harm (orben-przybylski windows; twenge trend asymmetry; nordic-ban policy-response heterogeneity), but a targeted search found no qualifying non-replication of the age/sex windows. Marked thin rather than padded; the strongest counter-reading (tiny averages) lives on n_assoc_magnitude and is crosslinked.
audit_notes.n_assoc_benefits: NAS-2024 is the only qualifying FOR cluster; Odgers-review carries the both-directions nuance as COMPLICATES. No credible AGAINST located — flagged thin, not manufactured.
audit_notes.n_trend_real: The measurement-artifact hypothesis is carried honestly as a COMPLICATES sourced from within a pro-real-increase paper (nordic-trend); a standalone quantitative artifact source did not qualify under the protocol at authoring time. Thin. Geographic (F-geo-3): GBD 2021 (204 countries) reframes the rise as high-income-concentrated (fastest in North America) with global rates broadly stable — the deterioration is a high-income phenomenon in the modelled burden data, not a worldwide one.
audit_notes.n_trend_attrib: Both FOR findings are the twenge cluster — the audit treats them as one body of work. Two independent clusters (NAS-2024, odgers-review) push AGAINST. This is the map's most conflation-prone claim and is double-crosslinked to n_assoc_causal. Geographic (F-geo-2): GBD 2021 adds that global adolescent-depression rates were stable 1990-2021 with the rise concentrated in high-income North America (EAPC 1.04 prevalence) while East/South Asia and the Caribbean declined — a globally-diffused exposure with a non-global outcome challenges the principal-driver claim.
audit_notes.n_policy_ban: The FOR slot is deliberately empty — no measured evidence that age bans reduce use existed at authoring time. The single AGAINST cluster (aus-ban-eval) is three months of a leaky rollout; the node is scoped to USE, not mental health, and flagged thin.
audit_notes.n_mech_sleep: Two strong meta-analyses, one per direction (carter-sleep-meta between-person; uq-withinperson-meta within-person). The disagreement is the design contrast itself — typed as an assumption-sensitivity crosslink.
audit_notes.n_assoc_causal: Counter-signal preserved: independent clusters AGAINST its own claim (NAS-2024, reduction-rct-pool) — evidence against was retained, not pruned.
audit_notes.n_policy_deact: Counter-signal preserved: independent clusters AGAINST its own claim (antwerp-abstinence-meta, reduction-rct-pool) — evidence against was retained, not pruned. Cluster note: reduction-rct-pool deliberately spans Ferguson 2024 AND its 2025 reanalysis — they analyse the same experiment pool and must not corroborate each other.
audit_notes.n_policy_school: Working papers (not yet peer-reviewed at authoring, flagged in caveats): Abrahamsson 2024 (nordic-ban), Figlio & Özek 2025 (nber-florida), Allcott-Gentzkow 2025 (allcott-gentzkow w33697).
